CCP 996.320. BASED ON THIS POSITION TAKEN BY THE MOVING PARTY ANY COURT ORDER IS SUPERFLUOUS TO THE RIGHTS ESTABLISHED BY STATUTE. MOVING PARTY PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE THAT THE OFFICER TO WHOM THE BOND WAS GIVEN REFUSED TO RELEASE SUCH BOND UPON NOTICE OF CANCELLATION HAVING BEEN PROVIDED. Motion For Order Withdrawing Undertakings
Off Calendar Feb-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER WITHDRAWING UNDERTAKINGS is moot. Pursuant to the moving party's supplemental brief, the moving party has the right to withdraw its undertaking by operation of law without a Court order "by giving a notice of cancellation of withdrawal to the officer to whom the bond was given in the same manner the bond was given." CCP 996.320. Based on this position taken by the moving party any Court order is superfluous to the rights established by statute. Moving party presented no evidence that the officer to whom the bond was given refused to release such bond upon notice of cancellation having been provided.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

DEFENDANT LOMBARD FLATS, LLC, KEARNY WASHINGTON LLC, JOANNE ENG's Motion To Consolidate Cases Filed By Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc Against Martin Eng.

Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Peter L. Kutrubes of the law offices of Peter L. Kutrubes (925-939-9600) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The court hears from defendant as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC's Motion For Sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.7 is GRANTED in large part. The cross-complaint filed by Lombard Flats, LLC is stricken as a sanction because there is no factual or legal support for the cross-complaint which is an impermissible collateral attack on a judgment by a party who is not a judgment debtor and lacks standing to vacate the judgment. No monetary sanctions are awarded in the court's discretion because there has been no showing that either Lombard Flats or its attorney filed the cross-complaint in subjective bad faith and the striking the cross-complaint is sufficient to remedy the violation of CCP 128.7(b)(2) and (3). Written order is signed and filed.

Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter L. Kutrubes, Esq., T:925.939.9600 ext.101, for defendant/moving party, on Court Call;
Mark Lapham, Esq., T:925.837.9007, for plaintiff/opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PIONEER 74 LOTS, LLC's Motion For Order From Relief From The June 23, 2017 Entry Of Default is DENIED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Plaintiff'S Counsel Demas (A.K.A. Dennis) Yan From Representation Of Legal Recovery, Llc
Off Calendar Apr-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Dennis Yan telephone number 415-867-5797 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Legal Recovery, LLC.
Hanlen Chang of Law Offices of Hanlen J. Chang telephone 415-757-3422 for and with Defendant Martin Eng.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S Motion To Disqualify Plaintiff's Counsel Demas (A.K.A. Dennis) Yan From Representation Of Legal Recovery, LLC is denied. Mr. Eng fails to meet his evidentiary burden to show that there is a substantial relationship between Mr. Yan's prior representation of him and this case. The Yoch and United Commercial Bank cases are not substantially related to this case. Mr. Eng also fails to establish that Mr. Yan acted as his counsel with regard to the loans/promissory notes in case number CGC-14-542378. The evidence that Mr. Eng asks the court to take judicial notice of is equivocal and does not show an attorney-client relationship. The declaration that Mr. Yan filed in that case to support the plaintiff's motion for summary judgment states that Mr. Yan was acting only as a broker for his parents. The court did not consider Mr. Eng's declaration submitted with the reply papers. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
